title: hieracium bupleuroides subsp. crinifolium
intro: Hieracium bupleuroides subsp. crinifolium, also known as a lesser Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoid and sesquiterpene content. The health benefits of Hieracium bupleuroides subsp. crinifolium include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be made into a tincture or used in herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
